title: dryopteris maranguensis
intro: Dryopteris maranguensis, also known as East African wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dryopteris maranguensis include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
